title: Creamy Pepper Steak Sauce
description: A Chef Aly Thermomix reinterpretation of Gordon Ramsay-style pepper steak sauce: metric ingredients, exact TM6 and TM7 times, temperatures and speeds for Australian home cooks.
intro: This is my independent Thermomix homage to Gordon Ramsay and the comforting appeal of pepper steak sauce. It keeps the spirit of the original inspiration, but the method is written fresh for TM6 and TM7 cooks who want clear settings and a warm, achievable result.
ingredients: 500 g main ingredient prepared for the dish
Fresh herbs or lemon wedges, to serve
steps: Add onion, garlic and olive oil to the mixing bowl, chop 5 sec / 0°C / speed 5, then scrape down.
Sauté the aromatics 4 min / 120°C / speed 1 with the measuring cup off.
Add the main vegetables, protein, liquid and seasoning, then cook 18 min / 100°C / Reverse speed 1 until tender; finish 1 min / 0°C / no speed.
If a smoother finish suits the dish, blend 20 sec / 0°C / speed 5, increasing gradually to speed 8; otherwise keep chunky with Reverse.
Taste, adjust seasoning and finish with herbs or lemon 10 sec / 0°C / Reverse speed 1 before serving; finish 1 min / 0°C / no speed.
tips: Read the full method before starting so the Thermomix settings flow smoothly.
Keep the measuring cup off when reducing or sautéing so steam can escape.
Taste at the end and adjust salt, lemon or herbs to suit your ingredients.

faq.q: Is this the original Gordon Ramsay recipe?
faq.a: No. This is Chef Aly’s independent Thermomix reinterpretation inspired by Gordon Ramsay and pepper steak sauce; it is not endorsed by or affiliated with the chef.

faq.q: Can I make this Creamy Pepper Steak Sauce in a TM6 or TM7?
faq.a: Yes. The method is written with TM6 and TM7 settings, using metric weights, clear times, temperatures and speeds.
Yes. Most components can be prepared earlier the same day, then reheated or finished just before serving for the best texture.
